name: nucliadb-utils

metadata.name: nucliadb-utils
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
# nucliadb util python library

- Nats driver
- FastAPI fixes
- S3/GCS drivers


# Install and run tests

```bash
pdm sync -d
make test
```

The start and end values are 0-based.

classes.name: nucliadb_utils.storages.object_store.ObjectStore

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.storages.object_store.ObjectStore.bucket_create
documentation:
Create a new bucket in the object storage. Labels the bucket with the given labels if provided.
Returns True if the bucket was created, False if it already existed.

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.storages.object_store.ObjectStore.bucket_exists
documentation: Return True if the bucket exists, False otherwise.

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.storages.object_store.ObjectStore.bucket_delete
documentation:
Delete a bucket in the object storage. Returns a tuple with two boolean values:
- The first one indicates if the bucket was deleted.
- The second one indicates if there was a conflict.

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.storages.object_store.ObjectStore.bucket_schedule_delete
documentation: Mark a bucket for deletion. The bucket will be deleted asynchronously.

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.storages.object_store.ObjectStore.upload_multipart_start
documentation: Start a multipart upload. May return the url for the resumable upload.

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.storages.object_store.ObjectStore.upload_multipart_append
documentation: Append data to a multipart upload. Returns the number of bytes uploaded.

Generic interface for object storage services.
This must NOT include any NucliaDB/Nuclia specific logic.

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.storages.storage.Storage.delete_resource
documentation:
Delete all storage keys related to a resource

Parameters:
- kbid: the knowledge box id
- uuid: the resource uuid
- max_parallel: the maximum number of parallel deletes

classes.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.ControlPlane

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.ControlPlane.create_index
documentation:
Create a new index in Pinecone. It can only create serverless indexes on the AWS us-east-1 region.
Params:
- `name`: The name of the index.
- `dimension`: The dimension of the vectors in the index.
- `metric`: The similarity metric to use. Default is "dotproduct".
- `serverless_cloud`: The serverless provider to use. Default is AWS us-east-1.
Returns:
- The index host to be used for data plane operations.

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.ControlPlane.delete_index
documentation:
Delete an index in Pinecone.
Params:
- `name`: The name of the index to delete.

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.ControlPlane.describe_index
documentation:
Describe an index in Pinecone.
Params:
- `name`: The name of the index to describe.

Client for interacting with the Pinecone control plane API.
https://docs.pinecone.io/reference/api/control-plane

classes.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.DataPlane

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.DataPlane.__init__
documentation:
Params:
- `api_key`: The Pinecone API key.
- `index_host_session`: The http session for the index host.
- `timeout`: The default timeout for all requests. If not set, the default timeout from httpx.AsyncClient is used.

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.DataPlane.stats
documentation:
Get the index stats.
Params:
- `filter`: to filter the stats by their metadata. See:
https://docs.pinecone.io/reference/api/2024-07/data-plane/describeindexstats

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.DataPlane.upsert
documentation:
Upsert vectors into the index.
Params:
- `vectors`: The vectors to upsert.
- `timeout`: to control the request timeout. If not set, the default timeout is used.

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.DataPlane._estimate_upsert_batch_size
documentation:
Estimate a batch size so that the upsert payload does not exceed the hard limit.
https://docs.pinecone.io/reference/quotas-and-limits#hard-limits

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.DataPlane.upsert_in_batches
documentation:
Upsert vectors in batches.
Params:
- `vectors`: The vectors to upsert.
- `batch_size`: to control the number of vectors in each batch.
- `max_parallel_batches`: to control the number of batches sent concurrently.
- `batch_timeout`: to control the request timeout for each batch.

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.DataPlane.delete
documentation:
Delete vectors by their ids.
Maximum number of ids in a single request is 1000.
Params:
- `ids`: The ids of the vectors to delete.
- `timeout`: to control the request timeout. If not set, the default timeout is used.

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.DataPlane.list_page
documentation:
List vectors in a paginated manner.
Params:
- `id_prefix`: to filter vectors by their id prefix.
- `limit`: to control the number of vectors fetched in each page.
- `pagination_token`: to fetch the next page. The token is provided in the response
   if there are more pages to fetch.
- `timeout`: to control the request timeout. If not set, the default timeout is used.

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.DataPlane.list_all
documentation:
Iterate over all vector ids from the index in a paginated manner.
Params:
- `id_prefix`: to filter vectors by their id prefix.
- `page_size`: to control the number of vectors fetched in each page.
- `page_timeout`: to control the request timeout for each page. If not set, the default timeout is used.

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.DataPlane.delete_all
documentation:
Delete all vectors in the index.
Params:
- `timeout`: to control the request timeout. If not set, the default timeout is used.

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.DataPlane.delete_by_id_prefix
documentation:
Delete vectors by their id prefix. It lists all vectors with the given prefix and deletes them in batches.
Params:
- `id_prefix`: to filter vectors by their id prefix.
- `batch_size`: to control the number of vectors deleted in each batch. Maximum is 1000.
- `max_parallel_batches`: to control the number of batches sent concurrently.
- `batch_timeout`: to control the request timeout for each batch.

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.DataPlane.query
documentation:
Query the index for similar vectors to the given vector.
Params:
- `vector`: The query vector.
- `top_k`: to control the number of similar vectors to return.
- `include_values`: to include the vector values in the response.
- `include_metadata`: to include the vector metadata in the response.
- `filter`: to filter the vectors by their metadata. See:
   https://docs.pinecone.io/guides/data/filter-with-metadata#metadata-query-language
- `timeout`: to control the request timeout. If not set, the default timeout is used.

Client for interacting with the Pinecone data plane API, hosted by an index host.
https://docs.pinecone.io/reference/api/data-plane

classes.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.PineconeSession

methods.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.PineconeSession._get_index_host_session
documentation:
Get a session for the given index host.
Cache http sessions so that they are reused for the same index host.

Wrapper class that manages the sessions around all Pinecone http api interactions.
Holds a single control plane session and multiple data plane sessions, one for each index host.

classes.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.FilterOperator
documentation:
Filter operators for metadata queries.
https://docs.pinecone.io/guides/data/filter-with-metadata#metadata-query-language

classes.name: nucliadb_utils.aiopynecone.client.LogicalOperator
documentation:
Logical operators for metadata queries.
https://docs.pinecone.io/guides/data/filter-with-metadata#metadata-query-language

functions.name: nucliadb_utils.tests.fixtures.storage_settings
documentation:
Useful fixture that returns the settings used in the generic `storage`
fixture.

This becomes useful when `storage` is overwritten programatically. Tests or
fixtures depending on generic storage settings can use this fixutre to get
the appropiate settings, being agnostic to the specific storage used.

functions.name: nucliadb_utils.asyncio_utils.run_concurrently
documentation:
Runs a list of coroutines concurrently, with a maximum number of tasks running.
Returns the results of the coroutines in the order they were scheduled.

classes.name: nucliadb_utils.asyncio_utils.ConcurrentRunner
documentation:
Runs a list of coroutines concurrently, with a maximum number of tasks running.
Returns the results of the coroutines in the order they were scheduled.
